member.name: T:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottiePlaybackOptions
member.summary: Configuration options for Lottie animation playback and rendering.

member.name: P:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottiePlaybackOptions.Path
member.summary: The source or path to the lottie animation JSON file.
member.remarks:
This can be a URL or a local path relative to the web root.
e.g. "./_content/Blazor.Lottie.Player/loading.json"
or "https://example.com/path/to/animation.json".

member.name: P:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottiePlaybackOptions.AutoPlay
member.summary: Gets or sets whether the animation should start playing automatically.
member.remarks: Default is true.

member.name: P:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottiePlaybackOptions.Renderer
member.summary: Gets or sets the type of Lottie animation to render.
member.remarks: Default is SVG for best feature support.

member.name: P:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottiePlaybackOptions.Loop
member.summary: Gets or sets the loop behavior for the animation.
member.remarks: Can be true (infinite loop), false (no loop), or a positive integer for a specific number of loops.

member.name: P:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottiePlaybackOptions.Direction
member.summary: Gets or sets the playback direction.
member.remarks: 1 for forward, -1 for reverse. Default is 1.

member.name: P:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottiePlaybackOptions.Speed
member.summary: Gets or sets the playback speed multiplier.
member.remarks: 1.0 is normal speed, 2.0 is double speed, 0.5 is half speed. Default is 1.0.

member.name: P:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottiePlaybackOptions.SetSubFrame
member.summary: Gets or sets whether to enable smooth frame interpolation for high refresh rate displays.
member.remarks: Default is true for smooth animations.

member.name: P:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottiePlaybackOptions.Quality
member.summary: Gets or sets the rendering quality level.
member.remarks: Default is High.

member.name: P:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottiePlaybackOptions.EnterFrameEvent
member.summary: Whether to enable the enterFrame event.
member.remarks: If CurrentFrameChangedFunc is null this should be false.

member.name: T:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottiePlayer
member.summary: A Blazor component for rendering and controlling Lottie animations using BodyMovin.
member.remarks: The component provides a flexible way to integrate Lottie animations into Blazor applications. It supports various customization options, including animation source, playback settings, and event callbacks for animation lifecycle events.

member.name: F:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottiePlayer.ElementId
member.summary: Represents the unique identifier for a Lottie element's surrounding div.

member.name: P:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottiePlayer.LottiePlayerModule
member.summary: The module used to control Lottie Player functionality.

member.name: P:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottiePlayer.Classname
member.summary: The class list for the surrounding div, which includes the base class and the Blazor Lottie Player class.

member.name: P:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottiePlayer.Stylename
member.summary: The style string for the surrounding div, which includes the base style and any user-defined styles.

member.name: P:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottiePlayer.MergedUserAttributes
member.summary: Returns the merged user attributes with sensible defaults, allowing user overrides.

member.name: P:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottiePlayer.Class
member.summary: The CSS class name(s) to apply to the container.

member.name: P:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottiePlayer.Style
member.summary: The inline CSS styles to apply to the component.

member.name: P:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottiePlayer.UserAttributes
member.summary: Sets a collection of user-defined HTML attributes represented as key-value pairs.
member.remarks: This property is typically used to provide additional HTML attributes during rendering such as ARIA accessibility tags, data attributes, or any other custom HTML attributes that you want to apply to the component.

member.name: P:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottiePlayer.Src
member.summary: Gets or sets the source or relative URL for the component's content.
member.remarks:
*Required* e.g.
Src="https://mudxtra.github.io/LottiePlayer/newAnimation.json"
Src="./wwwroot/lottie/newAnimation.json"

member.name: P:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottiePlayer.AutoPlay
member.summary: A value indicating whether the media should automatically start playing when loaded. If set to false, the media will not start playing until AnimationPaused is set to false.

member.name: P:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottiePlayer.Loop
member.summary: Gets or sets whether the animation should loop.

member.name: P:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottiePlayer.LoopCount
member.summary: Gets or sets the number of times a loop should occur. Requires to be true.
member.remarks: Default is 0 (infinite loop).

member.name: P:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottiePlayer.AnimationType
member.summary: Gets or sets the type of animation to be used for rendering the Lottie animation.
member.remarks: Defaults to .

member.name: P:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottiePlayer.AnimationDirection
member.summary: Gets or sets the direction in which the Lottie animation plays.
member.remarks:
Defaults to .
Changing this property after initialization will not change the playback direction of the animation, use

member.name: P:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottiePlayer.AnimationSpeed
member.summary: Gets or sets the speed of the animation.
member.remarks:
Defaults to 1.0 (normal speed).
Changing this property after initialization will not change the speed of the animation, use

member.name: P:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottiePlayer.CurrentFrameChangeFunc
member.summary: Called when the current frame changes, with custom filtering logic
member.remarks:
The function receives the current frame number and should return true if the event should be raised. This allows for custom throttling logic (e.g., only every 10th frame, only on specific frames, etc.)
Warning: Frame updates could happen as frequently as 30-60 times per second.
Defaults to null. is only raised if something is attached to the handler.
Example: frame => frame % 10 == 0 will raise the event every 10th frame.

member.name: P:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottiePlayer.CurrentFrameChanged
member.summary: Called when the current frame changes (only when returns true)

member.name: P:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottiePlayer.DOMLoaded
member.summary: Called when the Lottie player is initialized and the DOM is ready.

member.name: P:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottiePlayer.AnimationReady
member.summary: Called when the animation is fully loaded and ready for playback.

member.name: P:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottiePlayer.AnimationCompleted
member.summary: Called when the animation completes.

member.name: P:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottiePlayer.AnimationLoopCompleted
member.summary: Called when an animation loop completes.

member.name: P:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottiePlayer.AnimationOptions
member.summary: Additional Less Used Animation Options.
member.remarks: Refer to for defaults.

member.name: P:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottiePlayer.TotalAnimationFrames
member.summary: The total number of frames in the animation. This is set when the animation is loaded and ready for playback.

member.name: P:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottiePlayer.CurrentAnimationFrame
member.summary: Gets the current animation frame as a double-precision value. This is updated during playback by the EventCallback of CurrentFrameChanged.

member.name: P:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottiePlayer.IsAnimationLoaded
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether the animation has been loaded and is ready for playback.

member.name: P:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottiePlayer.IsAnimationPaused
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether the animation is paused.

member.name: P:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottiePlayer.IsAnimationStopped
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether the animation is stopped.

member.name: M:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottiePlayer.PlayAnimationAsync
member.summary: If the animation is not already playing, this method will start playing the animation.
member.remarks: Should not be called before OnAfterRenderAsync.
member.returns: if the animation was successfully started; otherwise, .

member.name: M:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottiePlayer.PauseAnimationAsync
member.summary: Pauses the currently playing animation in the Lottie player.
member.remarks: Should not be called before OnAfterRenderAsync.
member.returns: if the animation was successfully paused; otherwise, .

member.name: M:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottiePlayer.StopAnimationAsync
member.summary: Stops the currently playing animation asynchronously.
member.remarks: This method halts the animation playback if an animation is currently running. The method returns a boolean indicating whether the operation was successful.
member.returns: if the animation was successfully stopped; otherwise, .
member.exception: Thrown if the Lottie Player Module is not initialized.

member.name: M:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottiePlayer.SetSpeedAsync(System.Double)
member.summary: Asynchronously sets the playback speed for the Lottie animation.
member.param: The desired playback speed. Must be a positive value, where 1.0 represents normal speed.
member.exception: Thrown if the Lottie Player Module is not initialized.

member.name: M:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottiePlayer.SetDirectionAsync(Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottieAnimationDirection)
member.summary: Sets the playback direction of the Lottie animation asynchronously.
member.param: The desired playback direction of the animation. Must be a valid value.
member.exception: Thrown if the Lottie Player Module is not initialized.

member.name: M:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottiePlayer.InitializeLottieModule
member.summary: Disposes the LottiePlayer Module if it exists, cleaning up resources and references. Then re-initializes it with any updated options.

member.name: M:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottiePlayer.OnAfterRenderAsync(System.Boolean)
member.summary: OnAfterRenderAsync override

member.name: M:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottiePlayer.SetParametersAsync(Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.ParameterView)
member.summary: Override the SetParametersAsync method to handle component parameters.
member.param: The parameters submitted with the component

member.name: M:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottiePlayer.DisposeAsync
member.summary: Disposes the LottiePlayer component asynchronously, cleaning up resources and references.

member.name: T:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottiePlayerModule
member.summary: Provides functionality to control and manage a Lottie animation player in a Blazor application.
member.remarks:
This class interacts with a JavaScript module to initialize, play, pause, and stop Lottie animations within a specified HTML element. It also implements asynchronous disposal to clean up resources.
WARNING: This class does not load lottie javascript files, you must load those yourself or use the component.

member.name: P:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottiePlayerModule.CanExecute
member.summary: Indicates whether the module can execute javascript based on its current state.

member.name: M:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottiePlayerModule.#ctor(Microsoft.JSInterop.IJSRuntime,Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.ElementReference)
member.summary: The constructor for the LottiePlayerModule class.
member.param: The reference to the HTML element that the Lottie animation will be rendered in.

member.name: P:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottiePlayerModule.CurrentFrame
member.summary: The current time/frame as reported back after EventFrame events.

member.name: P:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottiePlayerModule.TotalFrames
member.summary: The total number of frames in the animation as returned by AnimationReady

member.name: M:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottiePlayerModule.InitializeAsync(Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottiePlaybackOptions)
member.summary: Initializes the Lottie player with the specified playback options.
member.remarks: This method imports the required JavaScript module and invokes the initialization logic for the Lottie player. It also ensures that the instance is not disposing and that the JavaScript runtime is available before loading the module.
member.param: The playback options to configure the Lottie player.
member.returns: if the initialization is successful; otherwise, .

member.name: M:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottiePlayerModule.PlayAsync
member.summary: Plays the Lottie animation.

member.name: M:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottiePlayerModule.PauseAsync
member.summary: Pauses the currently playing Lottie animation.

member.name: M:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottiePlayerModule.StopAsync
member.summary: Stops the currently playing Lottie animation.

member.name: M:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottiePlayerModule.GoToAndPlay(System.Double,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Moves the animation to the specified frame and starts playback from that point.
member.param: The frame number to move the animation to. Must be a non-negative value.
A boolean value indicating whether to force the animation to move to the specified frame even if it is already playing. to force the action; otherwise, .

member.name: M:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottiePlayerModule.GoToAndStop(System.Double,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Moves the animation to the specified frame and stops playback at that point.
member.param: The frame number to move the animation to. Must be a non-negative value.
A boolean value indicating whether to force the animation to move to the specified frame even if it is already playing. to force the action; otherwise, .

member.name: M:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottiePlayerModule.SetSpeedAsync(System.Double)
member.summary: Asynchronously sets the speed for the associated animation. 1 is normal speed, 2 is double speed, 0.5 is half speed, etc.
member.param: The speed value to set. Must be a non-negative number.

member.name: M:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottiePlayerModule.SetDirectionAsync(Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottieAnimationDirection)
member.summary: Asynchronously sets the playback direction for the associated animation.
member.param: The direction to play the animation. It can be forward or backward.

member.name: E:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottiePlayerModule.OnAnimationReady
member.summary: Triggers when the Lottie animation is ready to be played and loaded by the DOM.

member.name: E:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottiePlayerModule.OnDOMLoaded
member.summary: Triggers when the Lottie animation has fully loaded in the DOM.

member.name: E:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottiePlayerModule.OnEnterFrame
member.summary: Triggers when the Lottie animation enters a new frame.

member.name: E:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottiePlayerModule.OnComplete
member.summary: Triggers when the Lottie animation completes playing.

member.name: E:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottiePlayerModule.OnLoopComplete
member.summary: Triggers when the Lottie animation completes a loop.

member.name: M:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottiePlayerModule.AnimationReadyEvent(Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottiePlayerLoadedEventArgs)
member.summary: Invokes the event when the animation is ready. (DOMLoaded)

member.name: M:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottiePlayerModule.DOMLoadedEvent(Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottiePlayerLoadedEventArgs)
member.summary: Invokes the event when the DOM is fully loaded with the Lottie animation.

member.name: M:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottiePlayerModule.CompleteEvent
member.summary: Invokes the event when the animation completes.
member.remarks: This event is triggered when the animation has finished playing all frames and loops, if applicable. If it's set to loop indefinitely, this event will never be triggered.

member.name: M:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottiePlayerModule.LoopCompleteEvent
member.summary: Invokes the event when a loop completes.

member.name: M:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottiePlayerModule.EnterFrameEvent(Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottiePlayerEventFrameArgs)
member.summary: Invokes the event with the specified arguments.

member.name: M:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottiePlayerModule.DisposeAsync
member.summary: Disposes the Lottie player module and cleans up resources.

member.name: T:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottieAnimationOptions
member.summary: Represents the advanced configuration options for rendering Lottie animations.

member.name: P:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottieAnimationOptions.LottieSourceJs
member.summary: Gets or sets the URL of the Lottie JavaScript library used for rendering Lottie animations.

member.name: P:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottieAnimationOptions.AnimationQuality
member.summary: Gets or sets the quality level of the Lottie animation rendering. This is a global setting.
member.remarks: Defaults to . Higher quality levels may result in smoother animations but could increase resource usage.

member.name: P:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottieAnimationOptions.SmoothFrameInterpolation
member.summary: Gets or sets whether to enable smooth frame interpolation for high refresh rate displays.

remarks.para: When true (default), the animation will interpolate between keyframes to create smooth motion that adapts to your display's refresh rate (60Hz, 120Hz, etc.).

When false, the animation will only update on the original After Effects keyframes, which may appear choppy on high refresh displays but offers better performance and pixel-perfect reproduction of the original animation timing.

member.name: M:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottieAnimationOptions.Equals(System.Object)
member.summary: Determines whether the specified object is equal to the current instance.
member.remarks: Two instances are considered equal if they have the same , , and values.
member.param: The object to compare with the current instance.
member.returns: if the specified object is equal to the current instance; otherwise, .

member.name: M:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottieAnimationOptions.GetHashCode
member.summary: Returns a hash code for the current object.
member.remarks: The hash code is computed based on the values of the , , and properties. This ensures that objects with the same property values produce the same hash code.
member.returns: An integer hash code that represents the current object.

member.name: T:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottieAnimationDirection
member.summary: Specifies the playback direction for a Lottie animation.

member.name: F:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottieAnimationDirection.Forward
member.summary: Represents the forward direction an animation will play.

member.name: F:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottieAnimationDirection.Reverse
member.summary: Represents the reverse direction an animation will play.

member.name: T:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottieAnimationQuality
member.summary: Specifies the rendering quality level for Lottie animations.
member.remarks: Quality affects the precision of curve calculations and overall visual fidelity. Higher quality settings provide smoother curves and better visual accuracy but require more computational resources.

member.name: F:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottieAnimationQuality.Low
member.summary: Low quality rendering with basic curve precision.
member.remarks: Fastest performance with reduced visual fidelity. Best for simple animations or when performance is critical (mobile devices, multiple simultaneous animations).

member.name: F:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottieAnimationQuality.Medium
member.summary: Medium quality rendering with balanced precision and performance.
member.remarks: Good balance between visual quality and performance. Suitable for most general-purpose animations and typical web applications.

member.name: F:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottieAnimationQuality.High
member.summary: High quality rendering with maximum curve precision and visual fidelity.
member.remarks: Best visual quality with smooth, precise curves. Use for detailed animations where visual perfection is important. May impact performance on lower-end devices.

member.name: T:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottieAnimationType
member.summary: Specifies the type of Lottie animation to render.

member.name: F:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottieAnimationType.Svg
member.summary: Creates clean SVG elements with proper viewBox handling.
member.remarks: Best feature support and general purpose.

member.name: F:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottieAnimationType.Canvas
member.summary: Uses 2D context, clears canvas each frame, handles sizing automatically.
member.remarks: Performance option for complex animations, typically better for full screen or large animations.

member.name: F:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottieAnimationType.Html
member.summary: Creates HTML elements with CSS transforms.
member.remarks: Optional, generally slower with limited support for complex animations.

member.name: T:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottiePlayerEventFrameArgs
member.summary: Event arguments for the Lottie player loaded event.

member.name: P:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottiePlayerEventFrameArgs.Type
member.summary: Gets the ID of the event.

member.name: P:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottiePlayerEventFrameArgs.CurrentTime
member.summary: Gets the current frame of the animation as a zero-based index.

member.name: P:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottiePlayerEventFrameArgs.TotalTime
member.summary: Gets the total number of frames to be processed.

member.name: P:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottiePlayerEventFrameArgs.Direction
member.summary: The current direction of the Lottie animation playback.

member.name: T:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottiePlayerLoadedEventArgs
member.summary: Event arguments for the Lottie player loaded event.

member.name: P:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottiePlayerLoadedEventArgs.ElementId
member.summary: Gets the ID of the Lottie player element.

member.name: P:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottiePlayerLoadedEventArgs.CurrentFrame
member.summary: Gets the current frame of the animation as a zero-based index.

member.name: P:Blazor.Lottie.Player.LottiePlayerLoadedEventArgs.TotalFrames
member.summary: Gets the total number of frames processed.

member.name: T:Blazor.Lottie.Player.EnumExtensions
member.summary: Provides extension methods for working with enumeration types.
member.remarks: This class includes methods that enhance the functionality of enums, such as retrieving custom descriptions defined by the .

member.name: M:Blazor.Lottie.Player.EnumExtensions.ToDescription(System.Enum)
member.summary: Retrieves the description associated with an enumeration value.
member.remarks: This method is useful for obtaining human-readable descriptions of enumeration values when the is used to provide metadata for the values.
